revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (ja:だるま, en:Daruma doll, simple:Daruma doll); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/67736836 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==「目入れと障害者差別」について== 全体的に読みづらいですが、一体どういうところが障害者差別だといわれていて、それがどういった思い違いなのか、全く分からないので修正のしようがありません。結局なにが言いたい項目なのでしょうか--[[利用者:民宿|民宿]] 2011年1月28日 (金) 07:32 (UTC) 10日以上待っても回答が得られませんでしたので、当該項目を削除させていただきます。またなにかありましたらその時に検討しましょう--[[利用者:民宿|民宿]] 2011年2月10日 (木) 15:06 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == 外部リンク修正 == 編集者の皆さんこんにちは、 「[[だるま]]」上の6個の外部リンクを修正しました。[https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?diff=prev&oldid=67736835 今回の編集]の確認にご協力お願いします。もし何か疑問点がある場合、もしくはリンクや記事をボットの処理対象から外す必要がある場合は、こちらの[[meta:InternetArchiveBot/FAQ|FAQ]]をご覧ください。以下の通り編集しました。 *http://www.city.fuji.shizuoka.jp/hp/page000010600/hpg000010520.htm にアーカイブ（ https://web.archive.org/web/20140805234534/http://www.city.fuji.shizuoka.jp/hp/page000010600/hpg000010520.htm ）を追加 *http://www.kashiwashobo.co.jp/new_web/column/rensai/r03-40.html にアーカイブ（ https://web.archive.org/web/20160304220146/http://www.kashiwashobo.co.jp/new_web/column/rensai/r03-40.html ）を追加 *http://www.kashiwashobo.co.jp/new_web/column/rensai/r03-40.html にアーカイブ（ https://web.archive.org/web/20160304220146/http://www.kashiwashobo.co.jp/new_web/column/rensai/r03-40.html ）を追加 *http://www.shirakawadaruma.net/ にアーカイブ（ https://web.archive.org/web/20150105012515/http://shirakawadaruma.net/ ）を追加 *http://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/publication/rekihaku/176witness.html にアーカイブ（ https://web.archive.org/web/20132217024200/http://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/publication/rekihaku/176witness.html ）を追加 *http://www.amie.or.jp/daruma/ の書式設定/使用方法を修正 編集の確認が終わりましたら、下記のテンプレートの指示にしたがってURLの問題を修正してください。 ありがとうございました。—[[:en:User:InternetArchiveBot|'''InternetArchiveBot''']] ([[:en:User talk:InternetArchiveBot|バグを報告する]]) 2018年3月16日 (金) 15:37 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1312662663 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=C|1= {{WikiProject Japan|importance=high|religion=y|culture=y}} {{WikiProject Buddhism|importance=low}} {{WikiProject Toys|importance=low}} {{WikiProject Mythology|importance=low}} }} ==Article name== My [[electronic dictionary|denshijisho]] tells me that the correct romanization for だるま is dharma, based on the Sanskrit→English connection rather than the Japanese→English connection. I'm going to move the page and leave a note that daruma is also used. [[User:Dekimasu|Dekimasu]] 02:28, 8 October 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Controversial aspects == I'm confused as to why one section was removed for being uncited, leaving all the other unreferenced sections. This is not likely a high-traffic article, and to expect the request for a citation to be filled in two weeks is very ambitious. And as I said, the relevant section also appears in the Japanese Wikipedia article. Anyway, here is a citation that should be sufficient corroboration to readd the section. From the [[Mainichi Shimbun]], January 25, 2003: ４月の統一地方選挙を前に、視覚障害者団体の代表が２４日、当選祝いの 定番となっている「だるまの目入れ」を見直すよう与野党各党に申し入れた。 目入れの儀式が視覚障害者への差別を助長するとの理由からで、くす玉 割りなどでの代用を提案した。 申し入れたのは静岡県視覚障害者協会の萩原善次郎名誉会長ら。これまで も選挙のたびに、目入れをやめるよう呼びかけてきたが、長年続いた風習は 今も根強く残っている。萩原さんは、自民党の甘利明副幹事長らを個別に 訪ね、「目入れの風習は両目があって完全だという偏見の意識を育てることに なりかねない」などと訴えた。甘利氏は「趣旨は理解できる」と述べたという。 だるまは縁起物として選挙に欠かせない存在で、萩原さんもそれ自体を否定 しているわけではない。「目入れという痛々しい印象を与えるイベントを避けて、 くす玉割りなど、やさしく思いやりのある手段に変えていただきたい」と話して いる。 [[User:Dekimasu|Dekimasu]] 08:32, 21 January 2007 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Further sources == Lafcadio Hearn's ''A Japanese Miscellany'' has more information on Daruma dolls than the small bit I added. It would be a wonderful resource for anyone wanting to expand this article. It's available free at [http://www.archive.org/details/japanesemisc00hearrich Archive.org]. In addition, there are several promising journal articles at [http://scholar.google.com/scholar?hl=en&lr=&safe=off&q=daruma+doll&btnG=Search Google Scholar]. — [[User:BrianSmithson|BrianSmithson]] 08:50, 21 January 2007 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Okiagari-koboshi == I am confused as to why we would translate ''okiagari koboshi'' as "getting-up little priest" just to maintain the Japanese grammar structure. I really thought about this, and I replaced it with "little roly-poly monk" for several reasons. First, the "little" would always come before "getting-up" in English. Second, I'm not sure why we claim they are priests rather than monks when we have already stated that they are based on [[Bodhidharma]]. And finally, "getting-up" is a tedious adjective. The point is not that he is waking or that he is rising by himself, but that he is "rising again", which is what a roly-poly does. "Self-righting" would also be okay (i.e. "little self-righting monk"), as that would de-emphasize the personification of the toy. I don't understand why we would advocate a literal translation when that translation results in the emphasis of a foreign nature of the term. [[User:Dekimasu|Dekimasu]][[User talk:Dekimasu|が...]] 09:54, 14 February 2007 (UTC) :The reason I changed back is because the translation I used was from the sources of the [[okiagari-koboshi]] article. The dolls the roly-poly Daruma dolls are based on are evidently not supposed to be monks, but priests (although there may not be much difference in this case), so of your proposed translations, my only ''real'' problem is with using ''monk'' rather than ''priest''. The translation "getting-up little priest" is from Hearn, I believe, but like I said, the other translations are good bar ''monk'', I think. — [[User:BrianSmithson|Brian]] ([[user talk:BrianSmithson|talk]]) 10:19, 14 February 2007 (UTC) ::I think that mentioning Hearn's name for the ''okiagari koboshi'' would be interesting to include in that article, then, and can be a good source of raw information, but I don't think he is usually considered a reliable source anymore (in the real-world sense, not the Wikipedia sense). I don't have a particular problem with priest, and I agree that there isn't a real difference, but 法師 is equivalent to 坊主 (or 坊さん) and 坊主 is almost always translated as "monk". Neither priest nor monk fits very well, and both terms are used, apparently interchangeably, in the [[Zen]] article, so if you like priest, that's fine with me. [[User:Dekimasu|Dekimasu]][[User talk:Dekimasu|が...]] 12:45, 14 February 2007 (UTC) :::Sounds like a plan. Choose whichever translation you prefer, then, replacing ''monk'' with ''priest'', and we're golden. — [[User:BrianSmithson|Brian]] ([[user talk:BrianSmithson|talk]]) 12:49, 14 February 2007 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == More Details == I found this site: http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/daruma.shtml It is ridiculously detailed and well referenced. Definitely not all the information is needed, but the origin to the eye painting would be nice, the translation and origin of the "both eyes open" phrase, and the real start to the commercialization of Daruma. I would if I had time, but it looks like interesting research. There is a Mickey Daruma vinylmation made by Disney in Japan. — Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/86.70.116.86|86.70.116.86]] ([[User talk:86.70.116.86|talk]]) 23:54, 11 June 2015 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == References linking back to themselves! Please assist. == I'm sorry I can only point this out, but real-life limitations mean that's the best I can do, so please follow up if you can. The McFarland references (#16 & 17 as of this writing) in the [[Daruma_doll#Feminine_representations]] section are linked back to this same article instead of to Google Books or some other web site, or having no link at all. The syntax is one I haven't seen before. Also, I didn't check other linked refs for the same behavior. Thanks in advance! --[[User:Geekdiva|Geekdiva]] ([[User talk:Geekdiva|talk]]) 10:06, 12 August 2014 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == [[:File:Daruma doll, cut out, 03.jpg]] to appear as POTD soon == Hello! This is a note to let the editors of this article know that [[:File:Daruma doll, cut out, 03.jpg]] will be appearing as [[Wikipedia:picture of the day|picture of the day]] on December 13, 2017. You can view and edit the POTD blurb at [[Template:POTD/2017-12-13]]. If this article needs any attention or maintenance, it would be preferable if that could be done before its appearance on the [[Main Page]]. Thank you. — [[User:Crisco 1492|Chris Woodrich]] ([[User talk:Crisco 1492|talk]]) 03:47, 2 December 2017 (UTC) {{POTD/2017-12-13}} :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Etymology section? == The term itself, ''Daruma'', seems not to be explained in the article. I suspect it's simply the (Hindi?) word ''dharma'', resurfacing in Japanese? (I also suspect this is too obvious to mention, to anyone familiar with Japanese culture?!) I remember reading somewhere tht the Japanese loanword for ''typewriter'' (remember typewriters? - kind-of acoustic word-processors?) was ''tuparaita'' or similar. If I'm right about ''Daruma'' / ''dharma'', the point needs stating for those of us for whom it's not obvious. Any able to help? [[Special:Contributions/90.244.204.38|90.244.204.38]] ([[User talk:90.244.204.38|talk]]) 10:30, 31 May 2023 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Adding a section for inscriptions and their meanings == I find it weird that in spite of how prominently the inscription is featured on the dolls there is not even one mention of them or their meaninings in the whole article and while they aren't strictly necesary to include on the dolls I still feel like because it's such a common feature on them that including it could improve the article. '''Some common inscriptions:''' 福(フク/Fuku)—Blessing or fortune, 勝(かつ/Katsu)—Victory, 寿(ことぶき/Kotobuki)—Longevity, 金(かね/Kane)—Money. Though not all dolls are limited to just one character from what I could find and some feature custom iscriptions compared to the more generic and universal ones that are usually sold: https://mymodernmet.com/japanese-daruma-doll/, https://japan-avenue.com/blogs/japan/what-is-a-daruma-doll. [[User:EldritchEmpress|EldritchEmpress]] ([[User talk:EldritchEmpress|talk]]) 11:01, 14 June 2025 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == References vs Sources == Hello, This article seems to have a standard References section linked in the body of the article as normal and a nonstandard "Sources" section which is largely duplicating information already listed in the References section? Is there a need for a Sources section at all? imo it seems that we could just remove the duplicate listings that also reside in References and move the rest to Further Reading. Any thoughts on this? Thanks all! [[User:Shamra12|Shamra12]] ([[User talk:Shamra12|talk]]) 23:02, 21 September 2025 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Simple English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/7218724 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{dyktalk|4 September|2013}} {{translated page|en|Daruma doll|version=458703157}}

{{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/67736836 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==「目入れと障害者差別」について== 全体的に読みづらいですが、一体どういうところが障害者差別だといわれていて、それがどういった思い違いなのか、全く分からないので修正のしようがありません。結局なにが言いたい項目なのでしょうか--[[User:民宿|民宿]] 2011年1月28日 (金) 07:32 (UTC) 10日以上待っても回答が得られませんでしたので、当該項目を削除させていただきます。またなにかありましたらその時に検討しましょう--[[User:民宿|民宿]] 2011年2月10日 (木) 15:06 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == 外部リンク修正 == 編集者の皆さんこんにちは、 「[[だるま]]」上の6個の外部リンクを修正しました。[https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?diff=prev&oldid=67736835 今回の編集]の確認にご協力お願いします。もし何か疑問点がある場合、もしくはリンクや記事をボットの処理対象から外す必要がある場合は、こちらの[[meta:InternetArchiveBot/FAQ|FAQ]]をご覧ください。以下の通り編集しました。 *http://www.city.fuji.shizuoka.jp/hp/page000010600/hpg000010520.htm にアーカイブ（ https://web.archive.org/web/20140805234534/http://www.city.fuji.shizuoka.jp/hp/page000010600/hpg000010520.htm ）を追加 *http://www.kashiwashobo.co.jp/new_web/column/rensai/r03-40.html にアーカイブ（ https://web.archive.org/web/20160304220146/http://www.kashiwashobo.co.jp/new_web/column/rensai/r03-40.html ）を追加 *http://www.kashiwashobo.co.jp/new_web/column/rensai/r03-40.html にアーカイブ（ https://web.archive.org/web/20160304220146/http://www.kashiwashobo.co.jp/new_web/column/rensai/r03-40.html ）を追加 *http://www.shirakawadaruma.net/ にアーカイブ（ https://web.archive.org/web/20150105012515/http://shirakawadaruma.net/ ）を追加 *http://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/publication/rekihaku/176witness.html にアーカイブ（ https://web.archive.org/web/20132217024200/http://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/publication/rekihaku/176witness.html ）を追加 *http://www.amie.or.jp/daruma/ の書式設定/使用方法を修正 編集の確認が終わりましたら、下記のテンプレートの指示にしたがってURLの問題を修正してください。 ありがとうございました。—[[:en:User:InternetArchiveBot|'''InternetArchiveBot''']] ([[:en:User talk:InternetArchiveBot|バグを報告する]]) 2018年3月16日 (金) 15:37 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1312662663 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=C|1= {{WikiProject Japan|importance=high|religion=y|culture=y}} {{WikiProject Buddhism|importance=low}} {{WikiProject Toys|importance=low}} {{WikiProject Mythology|importance=low}} }} ==Article name== My [[electronic dictionary|denshijisho]] tells me that the correct romanization for だるま is dharma, based on the Sanskrit→English connection rather than the Japanese→English connection. I'm going to move the page and leave a note that daruma is also used. [[User:Dekimasu|Dekimasu]] 02:28, 8 October 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Controversial aspects == I'm confused as to why one section was removed for being uncited, leaving all the other unreferenced sections. This is not likely a high-traffic article, and to expect the request for a citation to be filled in two weeks is very ambitious. And as I said, the relevant section also appears in the Japanese Wikipedia article. Anyway, here is a citation that should be sufficient corroboration to readd the section. From the [[Mainichi Shimbun]], January 25, 2003: ４月の統一地方選挙を前に、視覚障害者団体の代表が２４日、当選祝いの 定番となっている「だるまの目入れ」を見直すよう与野党各党に申し入れた。 目入れの儀式が視覚障害者への差別を助長するとの理由からで、くす玉 割りなどでの代用を提案した。 申し入れたのは静岡県視覚障害者協会の萩原善次郎名誉会長ら。これまで も選挙のたびに、目入れをやめるよう呼びかけてきたが、長年続いた風習は 今も根強く残っている。萩原さんは、自民党の甘利明副幹事長らを個別に 訪ね、「目入れの風習は両目があって完全だという偏見の意識を育てることに なりかねない」などと訴えた。甘利氏は「趣旨は理解できる」と述べたという。 だるまは縁起物として選挙に欠かせない存在で、萩原さんもそれ自体を否定 しているわけではない。「目入れという痛々しい印象を与えるイベントを避けて、 くす玉割りなど、やさしく思いやりのある手段に変えていただきたい」と話して いる。 [[User:Dekimasu|Dekimasu]] 08:32, 21 January 2007 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Further sources == Lafcadio Hearn's ''A Japanese Miscellany'' has more information on Daruma dolls than the small bit I added. It would be a wonderful resource for anyone wanting to expand this article. It's available free at [http://www.archive.org/details/japanesemisc00hearrich Archive.org]. In addition, there are several promising journal articles at [http://scholar.google.com/scholar?hl=en&lr=&safe=off&q=daruma+doll&btnG=Search Google Scholar]. — [[User:BrianSmithson|BrianSmithson]] 08:50, 21 January 2007 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Okiagari-koboshi == I am confused as to why we would translate ''okiagari koboshi'' as "getting-up little priest" just to maintain the Japanese grammar structure. I really thought about this, and I replaced it with "little roly-poly monk" for several reasons. First, the "little" would always come before "getting-up" in English. Second, I'm not sure why we claim they are priests rather than monks when we have already stated that they are based on [[Bodhidharma]]. And finally, "getting-up" is a tedious adjective. The point is not that he is waking or that he is rising by himself, but that he is "rising again", which is what a roly-poly does. "Self-righting" would also be okay (i.e. "little self-righting monk"), as that would de-emphasize the personification of the toy. I don't understand why we would advocate a literal translation when that translation results in the emphasis of a foreign nature of the term. [[User:Dekimasu|Dekimasu]][[User talk:Dekimasu|が...]] 09:54, 14 February 2007 (UTC) :The reason I changed back is because the translation I used was from the sources of the [[okiagari-koboshi]] article. The dolls the roly-poly Daruma dolls are based on are evidently not supposed to be monks, but priests (although there may not be much difference in this case), so of your proposed translations, my only ''real'' problem is with using ''monk'' rather than ''priest''. The translation "getting-up little priest" is from Hearn, I believe, but like I said, the other translations are good bar ''monk'', I think. — [[User:BrianSmithson|Brian]] ([[user talk:BrianSmithson|talk]]) 10:19, 14 February 2007 (UTC) ::I think that mentioning Hearn's name for the ''okiagari koboshi'' would be interesting to include in that article, then, and can be a good source of raw information, but I don't think he is usually considered a reliable source anymore (in the real-world sense, not the Wikipedia sense). I don't have a particular problem with priest, and I agree that there isn't a real difference, but 法師 is equivalent to 坊主 (or 坊さん) and 坊主 is almost always translated as "monk". Neither priest nor monk fits very well, and both terms are used, apparently interchangeably, in the [[Zen]] article, so if you like priest, that's fine with me. [[User:Dekimasu|Dekimasu]][[User talk:Dekimasu|が...]] 12:45, 14 February 2007 (UTC) :::Sounds like a plan. Choose whichever translation you prefer, then, replacing ''monk'' with ''priest'', and we're golden. — [[User:BrianSmithson|Brian]] ([[user talk:BrianSmithson|talk]]) 12:49, 14 February 2007 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == More Details == I found this site: http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/daruma.shtml It is ridiculously detailed and well referenced. Definitely not all the information is needed, but the origin to the eye painting would be nice, the translation and origin of the "both eyes open" phrase, and the real start to the commercialization of Daruma. I would if I had time, but it looks like interesting research. There is a Mickey Daruma vinylmation made by Disney in Japan. — Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/86.70.116.86|86.70.116.86]] ([[User talk:86.70.116.86|talk]]) 23:54, 11 June 2015 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == References linking back to themselves! Please assist. == I'm sorry I can only point this out, but real-life limitations mean that's the best I can do, so please follow up if you can. The McFarland references (#16 & 17 as of this writing) in the [[Daruma_doll#Feminine_representations]] section are linked back to this same article instead of to Google Books or some other web site, or having no link at all. The syntax is one I haven't seen before. Also, I didn't check other linked refs for the same behavior. Thanks in advance! --[[User:Geekdiva|Geekdiva]] ([[User talk:Geekdiva|talk]]) 10:06, 12 August 2014 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == [[:File:Daruma doll, cut out, 03.jpg]] to appear as POTD soon == Hello! This is a note to let the editors of this article know that [[:File:Daruma doll, cut out, 03.jpg]] will be appearing as [[Wikipedia:picture of the day|picture of the day]] on December 13, 2017. You can view and edit the POTD blurb at [[Template:POTD/2017-12-13]]. If this article needs any attention or maintenance, it would be preferable if that could be done before its appearance on the [[Main Page]]. Thank you. — [[User:Crisco 1492|Chris Woodrich]] ([[User talk:Crisco 1492|talk]]) 03:47, 2 December 2017 (UTC) {{POTD/2017-12-13}} :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Etymology section? == The term itself, ''Daruma'', seems not to be explained in the article. I suspect it's simply the (Hindi?) word ''dharma'', resurfacing in Japanese? (I also suspect this is too obvious to mention, to anyone familiar with Japanese culture?!) I remember reading somewhere tht the Japanese loanword for ''typewriter'' (remember typewriters? - kind-of acoustic word-processors?) was ''tuparaita'' or similar. If I'm right about ''Daruma'' / ''dharma'', the point needs stating for those of us for whom it's not obvious. Any able to help? [[Special:Contributions/90.244.204.38|90.244.204.38]] ([[User talk:90.244.204.38|talk]]) 10:30, 31 May 2023 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Adding a section for inscriptions and their meanings == I find it weird that in spite of how prominently the inscription is featured on the dolls there is not even one mention of them or their meaninings in the whole article and while they aren't strictly necesary to include on the dolls I still feel like because it's such a common feature on them that including it could improve the article. '''Some common inscriptions:''' 福(フク/Fuku)—Blessing or fortune, 勝(かつ/Katsu)—Victory, 寿(ことぶき/Kotobuki)—Longevity, 金(かね/Kane)—Money. Though not all dolls are limited to just one character from what I could find and some feature custom iscriptions compared to the more generic and universal ones that are usually sold: https://mymodernmet.com/japanese-daruma-doll/, https://japan-avenue.com/blogs/japan/what-is-a-daruma-doll. [[User:EldritchEmpress|EldritchEmpress]] ([[User talk:EldritchEmpress|talk]]) 11:01, 14 June 2025 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == References vs Sources == Hello, This article seems to have a standard References section linked in the body of the article as normal and a nonstandard "Sources" section which is largely duplicating information already listed in the References section? Is there a need for a Sources section at all? imo it seems that we could just remove the duplicate listings that also reside in References and move the rest to Further Reading. Any thoughts on this? Thanks all! [[User:Shamra12|Shamra12]] ([[User talk:Shamra12|talk]]) 23:02, 21 September 2025 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Simple English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/7218724 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{dyktalk|4 September|2013}} {{translated page|en|Daruma doll|version=458703157}}

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] previous contributors were [[User:en>DrTitan28]], [[User:Immanuelle]], [[User:EmmaBot]], see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Daruma_doll here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/67736836 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==「目入れと障害者差別」について== 全体的に読みづらいですが、一体どういうところが障害者差別だといわれていて、それがどういった思い違いなのか、全く分からないので修正のしようがありません。結局なにが言いたい項目なのでしょうか--[[User:民宿|民宿]] 2011年1月28日 (金) 07:32 (UTC) 10日以上待っても回答が得られませんでしたので、当該項目を削除させていただきます。またなにかありましたらその時に検討しましょう--[[User:民宿|民宿]] 2011年2月10日 (木) 15:06 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == 外部リンク修正 == 編集者の皆さんこんにちは、 「[[だるま]]」上の6個の外部リンクを修正しました。[https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?diff=prev&oldid=67736835 今回の編集]の確認にご協力お願いします。もし何か疑問点がある場合、もしくはリンクや記事をボットの処理対象から外す必要がある場合は、こちらの[[meta:InternetArchiveBot/FAQ|FAQ]]をご覧ください。以下の通り編集しました。 *http://www.city.fuji.shizuoka.jp/hp/page000010600/hpg000010520.htm にアーカイブ（ https://web.archive.org/web/20140805234534/http://www.city.fuji.shizuoka.jp/hp/page000010600/hpg000010520.htm ）を追加 *http://www.kashiwashobo.co.jp/new_web/column/rensai/r03-40.html にアーカイブ（ https://web.archive.org/web/20160304220146/http://www.kashiwashobo.co.jp/new_web/column/rensai/r03-40.html ）を追加 *http://www.kashiwashobo.co.jp/new_web/column/rensai/r03-40.html にアーカイブ（ https://web.archive.org/web/20160304220146/http://www.kashiwashobo.co.jp/new_web/column/rensai/r03-40.html ）を追加 *http://www.shirakawadaruma.net/ にアーカイブ（ https://web.archive.org/web/20150105012515/http://shirakawadaruma.net/ ）を追加 *http://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/publication/rekihaku/176witness.html にアーカイブ（ https://web.archive.org/web/20132217024200/http://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/publication/rekihaku/176witness.html ）を追加 *http://www.amie.or.jp/daruma/ の書式設定/使用方法を修正 編集の確認が終わりましたら、下記のテンプレートの指示にしたがってURLの問題を修正してください。 ありがとうございました。—[[:en:User:InternetArchiveBot|'''InternetArchiveBot''']] ([[:en:User talk:InternetArchiveBot|バグを報告する]]) 2018年3月16日 (金) 15:37 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1312662663 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=C|1= {{WikiProject Japan|importance=high|religion=y|culture=y}} {{WikiProject Buddhism|importance=low}} {{WikiProject Toys|importance=low}} {{WikiProject Mythology|importance=low}} }} ==Article name== My [[electronic dictionary|denshijisho]] tells me that the correct romanization for だるま is dharma, based on the Sanskrit→English connection rather than the Japanese→English connection. I'm going to move the page and leave a note that daruma is also used. [[User:Dekimasu|Dekimasu]] 02:28, 8 October 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Controversial aspects == I'm confused as to why one section was removed for being uncited, leaving all the other unreferenced sections. This is not likely a high-traffic article, and to expect the request for a citation to be filled in two weeks is very ambitious. And as I said, the relevant section also appears in the Japanese Wikipedia article. Anyway, here is a citation that should be sufficient corroboration to readd the section. From the [[Mainichi Shimbun]], January 25, 2003: ４月の統一地方選挙を前に、視覚障害者団体の代表が２４日、当選祝いの 定番となっている「だるまの目入れ」を見直すよう与野党各党に申し入れた。 目入れの儀式が視覚障害者への差別を助長するとの理由からで、くす玉 割りなどでの代用を提案した。 申し入れたのは静岡県視覚障害者協会の萩原善次郎名誉会長ら。これまで も選挙のたびに、目入れをやめるよう呼びかけてきたが、長年続いた風習は 今も根強く残っている。萩原さんは、自民党の甘利明副幹事長らを個別に 訪ね、「目入れの風習は両目があって完全だという偏見の意識を育てることに なりかねない」などと訴えた。甘利氏は「趣旨は理解できる」と述べたという。 だるまは縁起物として選挙に欠かせない存在で、萩原さんもそれ自体を否定 しているわけではない。「目入れという痛々しい印象を与えるイベントを避けて、 くす玉割りなど、やさしく思いやりのある手段に変えていただきたい」と話して いる。 [[User:Dekimasu|Dekimasu]] 08:32, 21 January 2007 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Further sources == Lafcadio Hearn's ''A Japanese Miscellany'' has more information on Daruma dolls than the small bit I added. It would be a wonderful resource for anyone wanting to expand this article. It's available free at [http://www.archive.org/details/japanesemisc00hearrich Archive.org]. In addition, there are several promising journal articles at [http://scholar.google.com/scholar?hl=en&lr=&safe=off&q=daruma+doll&btnG=Search Google Scholar]. — [[User:BrianSmithson|BrianSmithson]] 08:50, 21 January 2007 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Okiagari-koboshi == I am confused as to why we would translate ''okiagari koboshi'' as "getting-up little priest" just to maintain the Japanese grammar structure. I really thought about this, and I replaced it with "little roly-poly monk" for several reasons. First, the "little" would always come before "getting-up" in English. Second, I'm not sure why we claim they are priests rather than monks when we have already stated that they are based on [[Bodhidharma]]. And finally, "getting-up" is a tedious adjective. The point is not that he is waking or that he is rising by himself, but that he is "rising again", which is what a roly-poly does. "Self-righting" would also be okay (i.e. "little self-righting monk"), as that would de-emphasize the personification of the toy. I don't understand why we would advocate a literal translation when that translation results in the emphasis of a foreign nature of the term. [[User:Dekimasu|Dekimasu]][[User talk:Dekimasu|が...]] 09:54, 14 February 2007 (UTC) :The reason I changed back is because the translation I used was from the sources of the [[okiagari-koboshi]] article. The dolls the roly-poly Daruma dolls are based on are evidently not supposed to be monks, but priests (although there may not be much difference in this case), so of your proposed translations, my only ''real'' problem is with using ''monk'' rather than ''priest''. The translation "getting-up little priest" is from Hearn, I believe, but like I said, the other translations are good bar ''monk'', I think. — [[User:BrianSmithson|Brian]] ([[user talk:BrianSmithson|talk]]) 10:19, 14 February 2007 (UTC) ::I think that mentioning Hearn's name for the ''okiagari koboshi'' would be interesting to include in that article, then, and can be a good source of raw information, but I don't think he is usually considered a reliable source anymore (in the real-world sense, not the Wikipedia sense). I don't have a particular problem with priest, and I agree that there isn't a real difference, but 法師 is equivalent to 坊主 (or 坊さん) and 坊主 is almost always translated as "monk". Neither priest nor monk fits very well, and both terms are used, apparently interchangeably, in the [[Zen]] article, so if you like priest, that's fine with me. [[User:Dekimasu|Dekimasu]][[User talk:Dekimasu|が...]] 12:45, 14 February 2007 (UTC) :::Sounds like a plan. Choose whichever translation you prefer, then, replacing ''monk'' with ''priest'', and we're golden. — [[User:BrianSmithson|Brian]] ([[user talk:BrianSmithson|talk]]) 12:49, 14 February 2007 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == More Details == I found this site: http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/daruma.shtml It is ridiculously detailed and well referenced. Definitely not all the information is needed, but the origin to the eye painting would be nice, the translation and origin of the "both eyes open" phrase, and the real start to the commercialization of Daruma. I would if I had time, but it looks like interesting research. There is a Mickey Daruma vinylmation made by Disney in Japan. — Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/86.70.116.86|86.70.116.86]] ([[User talk:86.70.116.86|talk]]) 23:54, 11 June 2015 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == References linking back to themselves! Please assist. == I'm sorry I can only point this out, but real-life limitations mean that's the best I can do, so please follow up if you can. The McFarland references (#16 & 17 as of this writing) in the [[Daruma_doll#Feminine_representations]] section are linked back to this same article instead of to Google Books or some other web site, or having no link at all. The syntax is one I haven't seen before. Also, I didn't check other linked refs for the same behavior. Thanks in advance! --[[User:Geekdiva|Geekdiva]] ([[User talk:Geekdiva|talk]]) 10:06, 12 August 2014 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == [[:File:Daruma doll, cut out, 03.jpg]] to appear as POTD soon == Hello! This is a note to let the editors of this article know that [[:File:Daruma doll, cut out, 03.jpg]] will be appearing as [[Wikipedia:picture of the day|picture of the day]] on December 13, 2017. You can view and edit the POTD blurb at [[Template:POTD/2017-12-13]]. If this article needs any attention or maintenance, it would be preferable if that could be done before its appearance on the [[Main Page]]. Thank you. — [[User:Crisco 1492|Chris Woodrich]] ([[User talk:Crisco 1492|talk]]) 03:47, 2 December 2017 (UTC) {{POTD/2017-12-13}} :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Etymology section? == The term itself, ''Daruma'', seems not to be explained in the article. I suspect it's simply the (Hindi?) word ''dharma'', resurfacing in Japanese? (I also suspect this is too obvious to mention, to anyone familiar with Japanese culture?!) I remember reading somewhere tht the Japanese loanword for ''typewriter'' (remember typewriters? - kind-of acoustic word-processors?) was ''tuparaita'' or similar. If I'm right about ''Daruma'' / ''dharma'', the point needs stating for those of us for whom it's not obvious. Any able to help? [[Special:Contributions/90.244.204.38|90.244.204.38]] ([[User talk:90.244.204.38|talk]]) 10:30, 31 May 2023 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Adding a section for inscriptions and their meanings == I find it weird that in spite of how prominently the inscription is featured on the dolls there is not even one mention of them or their meaninings in the whole article and while they aren't strictly necesary to include on the dolls I still feel like because it's such a common feature on them that including it could improve the article. '''Some common inscriptions:''' 福(フク/Fuku)—Blessing or fortune, 勝(かつ/Katsu)—Victory, 寿(ことぶき/Kotobuki)—Longevity, 金(かね/Kane)—Money. Though not all dolls are limited to just one character from what I could find and some feature custom iscriptions compared to the more generic and universal ones that are usually sold: https://mymodernmet.com/japanese-daruma-doll/, https://japan-avenue.com/blogs/japan/what-is-a-daruma-doll. [[User:EldritchEmpress|EldritchEmpress]] ([[User talk:EldritchEmpress|talk]]) 11:01, 14 June 2025 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == References vs Sources == Hello, This article seems to have a standard References section linked in the body of the article as normal and a nonstandard "Sources" section which is largely duplicating information already listed in the References section? Is there a need for a Sources section at all? imo it seems that we could just remove the duplicate listings that also reside in References and move the rest to Further Reading. Any thoughts on this? Thanks all! [[User:Shamra12|Shamra12]] ([[User talk:Shamra12|talk]]) 23:02, 21 September 2025 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Simple English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/7218724 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:08, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{dyktalk|4 September|2013}} {{translated page|en|Daruma doll|version=458703157}}
